events.title: Heber Valley Main to Main
events.description: Join us for an exciting race day! The 10K race starts at 7 a.m. from Heber City Park, taking you through beautiful Midway streets and back. The 5K race begins at 7:15 a.m. at the same location.

events.title: Wasatch Half Marathon and 10K
events.description: Come join us in the cool mountain scenery at The Wasatch Half Marathon and 10k! We are really to share this wonderful mostly trail run course to the race calendar. This event is one you don't want to miss. Starting and ending near the Soldier Hollow Nordic Center they’ll be no shortage of breathtaking scenery with the rewards of accomplishing something great!  No need for shuttles at this event as you’ll be able to park and the start/finish line.

events.title: Learn American Mahjong with Mahjong Hive
events.description: Join us for a relaxed, beginner-friendly evening where we’ll demystify the beautiful game of Mahjong. Come for good company, easy learning, and great snacks. events.title: Rock-n-Roll Train
events.description: The Rock-n-Roll Train is a 90 minute round trip ride on the historic Heber Train featuring great rock-n-roll music, trivia, and train history hosted by Mark Nelson, Executive Director. A great date night! You'll be singing along to some of the greatest rock hits ever. This year we will have a 21-and-older car with beer and wine available. You are not allowed to bring alcohol on the train! And, when the train returns you can stick around for a live concert featuring The Flashback Brothers. There will be food and drink and lots of good times at the Depot after the train! The onboard Train Bar will be open during the concert. Alcohol must be consumed on the train.

Doors open at 7pm, performances begin at 8pm. events.title: Midway Swiss Days
events.description: The annual Swiss Days Festival takes place in Midway, Utah, the Friday and Saturday before Labor Day each year and draws a huge crowd both days. Swiss Days includes a 10K race, parade, live entertainment, and so much more. DATES: Friday, September 4 & Saturday, September 5, 2026 SCHEDULE: Friday, Sep 4th: Market 8:00 am - 8:00 pm Saturday, Sep 5th: Market 8:00 am - 8:00 pm Breakfast & 10K Run start at 7:00 am, Parade at 10:00 am PARKING: Two public parking locations offer a free shuttle bus to and from the event location. Parking is $10 and the shuttle runs approximately every 15 minutes. There is limited street parking and private pay parking lots surrounding the square.‍ Complimentary bike parking is available at Centennial Park. PETS: Pets are NOT allowed at the venue.‍

events.title: Dominick Antonelli @ Cafe Galleria
events.description: Live Summer Music Series May through September (weather permitting) Every day but Mondays from 5:30pm – 8:30pm Sundays 10am – 1:30pm Dominick has been playing guitar for 21 years and writing original music for 15+ of those years. He looks at himself not as a guitar player, but an artist who utilizes the guitar and other forms to create an inspiring, healing, and contemplative experience for himself and listeners. Alternate tunings and intuitive playing have contributed immensely by shaping the instrumental and lyrical music that comes through him. He has a background in Integrative Health and is currently studying Music and Recording Arts Technology which is taking his creative possibilities to new depths and heights. He has performed at festivals in Thailand, Costa Rica, and the East Coast (US), as well as in yoga classes, local gigs, and workshops where he incorporates live music, meditation, group energy healing and writing into one experience. He loves performing live and sharing the gifts that continue to be cultivated. Read more about Dominick

events.title: Saturday Sunset Music Series
events.description: There’s no better way to spend a summer evening in Heber City than at the Saturday Sunset Music Series. Every Saturday from 7:00–8:30 PM, Main Street Park comes alive with live music, good food, and a relaxed, family-friendly atmosphere as the sun sets over downtown. Saturday programming is part of the broader Music on Main season. The series features a diverse lineup of local musicians, highlighting the depth of talent in the Heber Valley.
events.location: Heber City Main Street, Heber City, UT 84032

events.title: Wine Tasting %26 Cheese Train
events.description: Step aboard for a relaxed and thoughtfully curated Wine Tasting Experience on the train, where scenic rail travel meets a guided exploration of classic wines from around the world - sponsored by Vine Lore. Guests will enjoy a five-course wine tasting, each pour selected to showcase a range of styles and regions. The experience is designed to unfold at an easy, enjoyable pace, encouraging conversation, discovery, and time to savor each wine. Each table will be presented with a generous shared cheese board, available throughout the journey for guests to enjoy alongside the wines at their leisure. You'll ride in one of our beautiful vintage lounge cars. Tickets are sold either by the half table for 2 people (may be shared with 2 other guests), a full table for 4, and a limited number of single seats. Wines on the menu: Decoy Brut Cuvee Kumusha Sauvignon Blanc Cest La Vie Rouge (Pinot Noir & Syrah Blend) Larchago Tempranillo - Rioja, Spain Graham's Six Grapes Ruby Port
events.location: Heber Valley Railroad, 450 S 6th W, Heber City, UT 84032

events.title: Newcomers of Greater Park City Coffee Social & Speaker Event
events.description: DATE: Wed June 10 TIME: 9:30 AM-Light Brunch & Coffee Social; 10:15-Program begins PLACE: Park City Community Church, 4501 Highway 224, Park City, UT 8409 SPEAKER: Scott van Hartesvelt-Director of Leadership Park City Leadership Park City helps develop and connect the next generation of civic leaders in Summit County. The immersive curriculum focuses on civic education, leadership development and deep community connection. Since 2024, under Scott’s guidance, Leadership Park City has expanded public programming, continued deep community partnerships, and helped elevate conversations around the issues shaping Park City’s future. Scott’s background in tourism, entrepreneurship, and community leadership gives him a unique perspective on how people, place, and purpose unite to build a stronger Park City. This Newcomers of Greater Park City Area event is free and all are welcome – long-timers as well as newcomers to the area…and all in between! The Newcomers Club was established in 1995 as a not-for-profit entity to help all residents of Summit and Wasatch Counties learn about and become involved in their community through social, educational, sporting and travel activities. Visit https://parkcitynewcomers.org/ for more club details....
events.location: Park City Community Church, 4501 Hwy 224, Park City, UT

Come dressed to get dirty— muck boots, old clothes, and a towel or change of clothes are recommended!

events.title: Summit County Dems Fundraiser
events.description: Wednesday, June 24 6:00 to 8:00 PM The Red Banjo 322 Main Street, Park City Featuring Special Guest Brian King, Chair of the Utah Democratic Party We’re launching this fundraising campaign with a fun, informal evening featuring community conversation, food, and an opportunity to help build a stronger, more effective local political organization rooted in competence, integrity, and community-focused leadership. Tickets are $100 and include buffet pizza and a drink ticket. We also offer a no-questions-asked sliding scale because we believe civic participation should be accessible to everyone. Your support will directly fund voter outreach efforts here in Summit County at a time when outside political spending and coordinated campaigning are expected to play a larger role in local races. If you cannot attend, we hope you will still consider making a contribution in support of this work!...

events.title: Longevity, Simplified: What Actually Moves the Needle for Your Health
events.description: Onyx Club is proud to present a focused conversation on what truly drives long-term health, performance, and longevity. Join Dr. Timothy Duerler of Ovation Private Health and Connor Darnbrough, Co-founder of The Smart Fit Method for a focused discussion on the key drivers of longevity and health optimization. In this session they will break down the Four Pillars of Longevity – sleep, exercise, nutrition, and stress – and how they translate into real, measurable outcomes in your daily life. This talk is about understanding what actually works and how to apply it in a realistic, sustainable way. You’ll learn: How to focus on the key drivers that have the biggest impact on your health and longevity How advanced testing and diagnostics (lab work, body composition, VO₂ max, and more) can personalize your approach What data actually matters and how tracking the right metrics can improve performance and long-term health How to implement these principles within a busy, real-life schedule Connor will also share how these concepts come to life through the Smart Fit Method—a highly efficient, science-based training approach built from his own journey of injury, recovery, and rebuilding strength. After experiencing severe physical setbacks early in life, beginning his journey as a professional skier, he developed a system focused on helping others recover, perform, and extend their health span. Dr. Duerler brings over two decades of experience in family medicine, now focused on proactive, prevention-based care. His work centers on helping individuals preserve vitality, function, and cognitive performance through personalized, data-informed strategies. Together, they offer a grounded, practical perspective on how to take control of your health without overcomplicating it. The evening will include a brief presentation, followed by Q&A and time to connect with light bites and refreshments. Perks Attendees receive 15% off at the Overlook Restaurant at Black Rock Mountain Resort ...

events.title: June Listening Session
events.description: Explore the intersecting languages of quilting and synthesizers. On June 24, artists Mary Toscano and Andrew Rease Shaw lead one of two Listening Sessions. Engage with their collaborative project, LFO, currently on view in (Re)sounding. Experience Toscano’s quilts and the corresponding compositions by Shaw. The artists will discuss their project while inviting reflections gained from an evening of close viewing and deep listening.

events.title: Solstice Volunteer + Beer (21+)
events.description: Celebrate the solstice and longest day of the year with Swaner! Help us tackle invasive weeds on the Preserve and improve habitat quality and the wetland ecosystem. Meet at the Swaner EcoCenter: extension.usu.edu/swaner/directions Volunteers will head out onto the Preserve to pull invasive weeds, and afterwards head back to the EcoCenter to enjoy the back deck from 7 – 8pm. Pizza, beer, and non-alcoholic seltzers will be provided to all volunteers. YOU MUST REGISTER TO ATTEND the event and be 21 years of age or older. ID’s will be checked at the door.
events.location: Swaner Preserve and EcoCenter, 1258 Center Drive, Park City

events.title: Plein Air Painting: The Living Landscape
events.description: Join painter Johnny DeFeo for three immersive days of plein air exploration, where landscape becomes both subject and teacher. Rather than a traditional “paint-along,” instruction is intentionally intimate and responsive, with frequent mini-critiques and one-on-one guidance. With short lectures and demonstrations all on location, develop your own visual language and body of work. Participants should bring their own supplies and have prior painting experience. This workshop is designed for artists ready to deepen their relationship with landscape and push their work beyond observation into expression....

events.title: Thrive | FREE Youth Well-Being Skills Summer Program
events.description: Thrive is a FREE evidence-based program equipping Summit & Wasatch County students in grades 6-12 with vital life skills. Imagine our youth… Confidently managing stress amidst school pressures and social uncertainties. Nurturing positive relationships built on empathy and effective communication. Making healthy choices for their physical and mental well-being. Thrive empowers them with these skills and more! Students meet for one hour weekly on Zoom, with a cohort of up to ten peers and a Thrive facilitator, to learn, practice, and reflect on valuable well-being skills. events.title: Live PC Give PC
events.description: Live PC Give PC will take place on FRIDAY, NOVEMBER 6, 2026! HELP SHAPE PARK CITY’S FUTURE IN A SINGLE DAY. Every November, Live PC Give PC brings our community together through LivePCGivePC.org for 24 hours of extreme generosity. Children break open their piggy banks. Young people dip into their savings. Businesses kick in matching grants. With everyone in the community giving whatever they can, we’ve raised millions of dollars for local nonprofits. It’s how we’re working together to preserve the place we love—so it will continue to thrive for generations to come.

events.location: Oakley Outdoor Arena, 960 W Center Street, Oakley, UT 84055
events.description: Junior Rodeo and Kids Activities at the Oakley Outdoor Arena. Treasure Dig, Arena Race, and Chicken Chase are free and open to the public for ages 12 & under. Sheep & Steer riding to follow.

events.title: Oakley Rodeo Men's Slack Events
events.description: Men's slack events ahead of the Oakley PRCA Rodeo: Tie Down, Steer Wrestling, and Team Roping at the Oakley Outdoor Arena.

events.title: Oakley Rodeo Women's Slack Events
events.description: Women's slack events ahead of the Oakley PRCA Rodeo: Barrels and Breakaway at the Oakley Outdoor Arena.

events.title: 3rd Annual Oakley Pickleball Tournament
events.location: Oakley City Recreational Complex, Oakley, UT 84055
events.description: 3rd Annual Oakley Pickleball Tournament — June 26 (Friday) and June 27 (Saturday). Registration $35 (includes first event), second event $10. Registration opened March 23rd, closes June 12th. Start times posted once brackets are made.

events.title: Tough Enough to Wear Pink Dinner & Auction
events.description: Annual Tough Enough to Wear Pink Dinner & Auction at the Oakley Red Barn — a community fundraiser raising awareness and funds for cancer research.

events.title: Latino Arts Festival
events.description: June 12-14, 2026 | Canyons Village Forum | Friday: 4-9:30pm, Saturday: 10am-9:30pm, Sunday: 10am-6pm A multi-day celebration of Latino arts and culture, featuring live performances, visual arts, film screenings, and culinary experiences. This annual event highlights local and international artists while fostering cultural connection and community engagement.

events.title: American Indian Services Native Market Days
events.description: Native Market Days | July 10–11, 2026 Canyons Village | Park City, Utah Native Market Days is a signature cultural experience hosted by American Indian Services, celebrating Native art, culture, fashion, dance, storytelling, and community. Held at Canyons Village in Park City, this event brings together Native artists, performers, designers, families, community members, and visitors in a vibrant setting that honors creativity, connection, and cultural expression. This two-day event will feature a Native Market, hoop dance performances, an art competition and judging experience, community gatherings, and a Native Fashion Show. Sponsors and partners are invited to help elevate Native voices, support Native artists and entrepreneurs, and align their brand with a meaningful cultural experience rooted in respect, representation, and celebration. Event Schedule Friday, July 10, 2026 * Native Market 10:00 AM–5:00 PM Sundial Pavilion * Hoop Dance Performances 11:00 AM–5:00 PM Forum Stage, Canyons Village Saturday, July 11, 2026 * Native Market 10:00 AM–5:00 PM Canyons Village * Hoop Dance Performances 10:00 AM–5:00 PM Forum Stage, Canyons Village * Native Fashion Show 4:00 PM–8:00 PM Kokopelli Grand Ballroom...
events.location: Canyons Village, 4000 Canyons Resort Dr, Park City
